scenario_name: Complete operational authorisation pack submitted
given: An applicant has assembled a complete Operational Authorisation pack with all required artefacts (ConOps, ODD, SORA evidence, insurance, crew credentials, etc.)
when: The applicant submits the pack to the CAA case officer intake system
then: The system accepts the submission, opens a valid case, and transitions to Completeness Check (F02)
and: A unique case ID is generated, submitted timestamp is recorded, and case officer receives notification

scenario_name: Pack passes automated completeness validation
given: A submitted pack undergoes automated validation against the completeness checklist
when: All required artefacts (ConOps, ODD, DAA evidence, insurance, crew training, etc.) are present
then: System flags pack as COMPLETE and notifies case officer that case can proceed to SORA assessment
and: Case status becomes INTAKE_COMPLETE; timestamp recorded

scenario_name: Pack missing critical artefact detected
given: A submitted pack is missing a critical artefact (e.g., SORA evidence or crew credentials)
when: Automated completeness check runs
then: System flags the specific missing artefact, sets case to RETURN_FOR_CLARIFICATION status, and generates coaching feedback
and: Applicant receives structured feedback identifying exactly what is missing and deadline for resubmission

scenario_name: Pack partially submitted, applicant in coaching mode
given: An applicant in Applicant Intake Mode has submitted partial artefacts (e.g., ConOps draft, incomplete SORA evidence)
when: Applicant requests coaching feedback before formal submission
then: System provides targeted coaching on what is missing and quality issues without rejecting the case
and: Applicant retains ability to revise and resubmit before formal INTAKE_COMPLETE flag

scenario_name: Pack re-submitted after return-for-clarification
given: A pack was returned for clarification due to missing artefacts; applicant has resubmitted revised pack
when: Second completeness check runs on resubmitted pack
then: System validates all previously flagged items are now present and case transitions to INTAKE_COMPLETE
and: Case officer is notified of successful re-submission; version history shows both original and revised submissions

scenario_name: Swarm-aware SORA evidence present, defensible SAIL derived
given: Pack contains swarm-aware SORA evidence demonstrating coordinated multi-aircraft behaviour, inter-vehicle link robustness, and collective decision-making limits
when: SORA assessor reviews evidence and applies SORA 2.0 methodology with swarm context
then: SAIL (Specific Assurance and Integrity Level) and OSO (Operational Safety Objectives) are derived defensibly
and: Assessment record is created with clear traceability to swarm-specific evidence; SAIL/OSO documented in case file

scenario_name: Swarm evidence sparse, SAIL cannot be auto-derived
given: Pack contains standard single-aircraft SORA evidence but lacks swarm-specific evidence on coordinated de-confliction and collective behaviour limits
when: SORA assessor attempts to derive SAIL using swarm context
then: System flags insufficient swarm-aware evidence; case marked for expert panel review rather than auto-green
and: SORA assessor documents reason for manual escalation; applicant is notified of evidence gap

scenario_name: SORA assessment requires re-assessment after ConOps revision
given: Initial SORA/SAIL derived; ConOps subsequently revised during phased trial (e.g., increased swarm size or autonomy scope)
when: Case gates to next phase with new operational envelope
then: System triggers re-assessment of SORA with updated ConOps; previous SAIL may become invalid
and: Assessment history captures both original and revised SAIL determinations

scenario_name: Clear ConOps and ODD reviewed, nominal and worst-credible behaviour judged
given: Pack contains detailed ConOps (flight profiles, crew roles, decision procedures) and ODD (environmental limits, GNSS availability, airspace constraints)
when: Safety-case reviewer assesses ConOps and ODD clarity
then: Reviewer confirms understanding of nominal swarm behaviour and identifies credible worst-case scenarios (link loss, geofence breach, non-cooperative encounter)
and: ConOps/ODD marked APPROVED; documented in case file with reviewer sign-off

scenario_name: ConOps ambiguous or missing failure mode procedures
given: ConOps submitted is vague on crew takeover procedures or contingency triggers
when: Safety-case reviewer reads ConOps
then: Reviewer cannot confidently judge worst-credible behaviour; flags ConOps as INCOMPLETE
and: Applicant is requested to provide detailed failure mode procedures (e.g., link-loss response, geofence-trigger RTL) before proceeding

scenario_name: ODD boundary unclear (GNSS-denied corner case)
given: ConOps states operation in urban area with periodic GNSS loss; ODD does not explicitly define GNSS-denial procedures or swarm de-confliction alternatives
when: Safety reviewer assesses coverage of worst-credible GNSS-loss scenario
then: Reviewer identifies gap: ODD does not bound behaviour during GNSS-denial; flag for clarification
and: Applicant must specify either: (a) geospatial exclusion of GNSS-poor zones, or (b) autonomous backup procedure (e.g., inertial-estimate + inter-vehicle ranging)

scenario_name: TDA approved, consultation package accepted, airspace segregated
given: Applicant has submitted TDA request with airspace coordinates, time-window, and consultation package identifying stakeholders and prior-notification plan
when: Airspace regulator reviews TDA request and consultation evidence
then: TDA is approved, NOTAM issued, and stakeholder consultation recorded; trial airspace is segregated from civilian traffic
and: TDA coordinates pinned to case file; NOTAM activation datetime set; consultation log archived

scenario_name: TDA conflicts with existing airspace user or procedure
given: TDA request overlaps with existing procedure box or military airspace training corridor
when: Airspace regulator checks TDA against current airspace utilization
then: Conflict detected; TDA cannot be approved as requested; regulator negotiates with conflicting user or proposes alternative time-window
and: Case placed on hold; applicant is informed of conflict and offered remediation options

scenario_name: Consultation package incomplete - stakeholders not identified
given: Consultation package does not identify nearby landowners, schools, or noise-sensitive sites per CAP 1616
when: Airspace regulator reviews stakeholder list
then: Regulator flags consultation package as incomplete; case cannot proceed without comprehensive stakeholder identification
and: Applicant is directed to conduct prior-notification planning per CAP 1616; case placed in RETURN_FOR_CLARIFICATION

scenario_name: Fly-away detected inside tolerance, automatic RTL triggered
given: Swarm aircraft drifts toward geofence boundary during autonomous flight; aircraft deviation from authorized area is within 50m tolerance
when: Onboard geofence enforcement system detects boundary approach
then: Automatic Return-to-Launch (RTL) is triggered; all swarm members initiate coordinated return
and: RTL execution is logged; case officer receives post-flight alert; incident marked as GEOFENCE_RTL_TRIGGERED

scenario_name: Fly-away detected beyond tolerance, flight termination activated
given: Swarm aircraft deviates from authorized area beyond 200m (configured tolerance); containment is at risk
when: Onboard geofence system detects breach beyond tolerance threshold
then: Independent flight termination system is activated (independent of C2 link); all swarm members initiate rapid descent and safe power-down
and: Termination execution logged in forensic data; case officer alerted immediately; post-flight investigation triggered

scenario_name: Geofence system itself fails - no containment available
given: Geofence enforcement system experiences hardware/software failure; boundary checking is unavailable
when: Swarm enters autonomous flight phase with failed geofence
then: System detects geofence unavailability and initiates CONTINGENCY_MODE_ACTIVATION
and: Crew is immediately notified of geofence loss; flight is aborted and aircraft diverted to safe landing zone per ConOps contingency

scenario_name: Multiple geofence boundaries (nested zones) with different trigger actions
given: Geofence configured with three zones: (1) primary trial boundary, (2) intermediate caution zone 100m inside, (3) inner safe-zone with crew stations
when: Aircraft trajectory approaches each zone boundary
then: Zone-specific actions triggered: caution zone → reduced autonomy + operator alert; primary boundary → RTL; breach → termination
and: Scenario data:
scenario_data:
| Zone | Boundary | Trigger | Action | |
| Caution | -100m from boundary | Entry | Alert + reduced speed |
| Primary | 0m (authorized limit) | Approach | RTL sequence initiated |
| Breach | +100m beyond primary | Detect | Flight termination |

scenario_name: C2 link momentary loss, ride-through successful
given: C2 link experiences momentary outage (100ms–1s) during autonomous swarm flight due to electromagnetic interference or multipath fading
when: Link loss detected by onboard monitor; swarm continues autonomous operation
then: Swarm members rely on inter-vehicle links and pre-planned autonomy; link recovery occurs within hold-safe timeout
and: Link loss event logged; case officer receives post-flight notification of outage duration and recovery; no safety impact

scenario_name: C2 link sustained loss, contingency mode activated
given: C2 link remains unavailable for > 10 seconds (configured threshold) during flight
when: Loss duration exceeds hold-safe timeout
then: Swarm enters CONTINGENCY_MODE: autonomous navigation via pre-planned waypoints or inter-vehicle consensus continues until RTL is triggered
and: Flight is diverted to predetermined safe landing zone; crew is unable to command but swarm remains safe and recoverable

scenario_name: C2 link loss during swarm consensus maneuver (high-risk branch)
given: Swarm is executing distributed consensus maneuver (e.g., coordinated altitude change); C2 link is lost
when: Loss occurs mid-maneuver when inter-vehicle coordination is critical
then: Swarm members lose ground control authority but maintain inter-vehicle links; they follow distributed contingency algorithm to safely freeze maneuver
and: Post-flight analysis shows whether consensus algorithm successfully prevented collision; high-risk scenario documented in lessons-learned

scenario_name: C2 link non-interference with other spectrum users verified
given: C2 system transmits on authorized frequency with approved power level; spectrum monitoring equipment is in place
when: Spectrum specialist reviews monitoring data and adjacent-frequency user complaints
then: No harmful interference detected; UAS emissions are within approved limits; spectrum coexistence confirmed
and: Monitoring report generated; compliance verified in case file

scenario_name: Inter-vehicle link failure between swarm members
given: One aircraft loses RF link to its neighboring swarm members but maintains C2 link to ground
when: Inter-vehicle link timeout occurs
then: Affected aircraft switches to ground-only contingency mode; ground crew re-establishes command authority
and: Remaining swarm members continue coordinated operation; separated aircraft is safely recovered or redirected

scenario_name: Cooperative encounter resolved predictably per DO-365
given: Swarm encounters another aircraft equipped with transponder (cooperative); position, altitude, and velocity known via ADS-B
when: DAA system detects cooperative intruder and initiates avoidance
then: Avoidance maneuver (altitude change or lateral displacement) is executed autonomously; safe separation is maintained per DO-365 standards
and: Encounter logged with intruder track data; post-flight analysis confirms resolution was predictable and safe

scenario_name: Non-cooperative encounter resolved, avoidance successful
given: Swarm encounters an aircraft without transponder (non-cooperative, position unknown); detected only by onboard radar/vision
when: DAA system senses intruder and initiates evasive maneuver
then: Swarm executes conservative avoidance (e.g., altitude climb); encounter is resolved without collision
and: Post-flight analysis shows conservatism was sufficient; encounter logged as resolved safely despite information asymmetry

scenario_name: Multi-agent swarm encounter during coordinated maneuver
given: Swarm is executing formation maneuver; external intruder is detected; swarm must simultaneously avoid intruder and maintain internal separation
when: DAA triggers avoidance; swarm consensus algorithm must coordinate avoidance without internal collision
then: Coordinated de-confliction ensures swarm members all execute compatible avoidance; safe separation maintained both internally and from intruder
and: Encounter logged with inter-aircraft separation metrics; demonstrates coordinated swarm DAA capability

scenario_name: Encounter in GNSS-denied environment (corner case)
given: Swarm is operating in GNSS-denied area (urban canyon, indoor); DAA is primary separation mechanism
when: Encounter is detected via onboard radar/vision in GNSS-denied corner case
then: DAA relies on local sensor data only (no GPS-aided fusion); avoidance is executed conservatively
and: Post-flight analysis shows whether GNSS-denial impacts avoidance confidence; logs captured for forensic review

scenario_name: DAA system failure - no avoidance capability
given: Onboard DAA system experiences hardware/sensor failure; encounter detection is unavailable
when: Intruder approaches swarm (cooperative or non-cooperative)
then: System detects DAA unavailability; flight is immediately aborted and swarm is diverted to nearest safe landing
and: Crew is alerted; incident is investigated; case may require DAA system redesign or additional operational constraints

scenario_name: Distributed consensus decision-making validated, emergent behaviour predictable
given: Swarm architecture is distributed consensus-based (no single lead); airborne limits enforce velocity, altitude, and heading constraints; emergent behaviour test data demonstrates predictability
when: Autonomy reviewer assesses architecture documentation and test evidence
then: Reviewer confirms collective behaviour meets ODD bounds; emergent phenomena (e.g., adaptive formation tightening) are explained and modeled
and: Architecture marked APPROVED; emergent behaviour evidence linked to case file

scenario_name: Airborne limits insufficient to prevent undesired emergent behaviour
given: Swarm architecture enforces local velocity/altitude limits; test evidence shows undesired emergent behaviour (e.g., collective oscillation, deadlock in consensus)
when: Autonomy reviewer analyzes emergent behaviour test results
then: Undesired behaviour identified; reviewer flags architecture as INCOMPLETE
and: Applicant must either: (a) redesign consensus algorithm, (b) tighten airborne limits further, or (c) add ground-based override mechanism

scenario_name: Lead-aircraft centralized architecture with failover to ground control
given: Swarm architecture uses designate lead aircraft for decision-making; failover protocol transfers authority to ground crew if lead is incapacitated
when: Autonomy reviewer assesses lead-aircraft architecture and failover procedure
then: Reviewer validates that leadership failover maintains swarm cohesion and prevents collision during transition
and: Failover procedure documented; test results confirm safe transition; architecture approved

feature: F10: ML/AI Assurance Case & Model Manifest
scenario_name: AMLAS case present, frozen-model manifest locked, explainability evidence available
given: Pack includes AMLAS/SACE assurance case for ML components (e.g., environmental classification, traffic prediction); frozen-model manifest documents exact model version, training data, and performance metrics; explainability framework (SHAP, attention maps) is documented
when: ML assurance specialist reviews AMLAS case and manifest
then: Reviewer verifies model version is pinned and reproducible; explainability evidence supports forensic audit trail
and: ML assurance marked APPROVED; manifest stored in immutable archive; explainability evidence linked to case file

feature: F10: ML/AI Assurance Case & Model Manifest
scenario_name: ML decision made, explainability evidence absent - forensic gap
given: Swarm uses ML model for real-time decision-making (e.g., autonomy mode selection); incident occurs; post-incident investigation requires forensic explanation of ML decision
when: Investigator attempts to trace why model made specific decision
then: Explainability evidence is absent or sparse; investigator cannot confidently explain decision to regulator
and: Case marked CRITICAL FINDING: ML forensic explainability is insufficient; design must include per-decision explanation logging

feature: F10: ML/AI Assurance Case & Model Manifest
scenario_name: Frozen-model manifest version mismatch detected post-trial
given: Case closes; post-trial audit discovers deployed model version differs from manifest-documented version
when: Audit compares manifest record to actual deployed code
then: Version mismatch flagged; case integrity questioned; forensic confidence reduced
and: Finding logged; lessons-learned note added regarding manifest-synchronization procedures; process improvement recommended

scenario_name: Crew workload demonstrated within acceptable bounds, takeover viable
given: Human factors assessment shows crew mental workload (NASA-TLX or equivalent) remains below 70% during nominal and off-nominal scenarios; take-over procedure requires < 3 seconds to resume command
when: Human factors inspector reviews workload assessment and take-over procedure documentation
then: Inspector confirms meaningful human control is demonstrated; crew can perceive, understand, and intervene in safety-critical scenarios
and: Human oversight marked APPROVED; workload metrics documented in case file

scenario_name: Crew workload exceeds threshold during multi-agent encounter
given: Simulation/test data shows crew workload exceeds 85% during simultaneous encounter + contingency scenario
when: Human factors inspector analyzes workload data
then: Inspector flags workload as EXCESSIVE; meaningful human control is questioned for this scenario
and: Applicant must either: (a) simplify crew procedures, (b) add automation to reduce workload, or (c) restrict operation to exclude this scenario from trial envelope

scenario_name: Take-over procedure undefined or untested
given: ConOps references crew take-over capability but does not specify procedure; no test evidence of actual take-over feasibility
when: Human factors inspector requests take-over procedure and test results
then: Applicant cannot provide tested procedure; take-over time and success rate remain unverified
and: Case marked INCOMPLETE; meaningful human control cannot be demonstrated; procedural redesign required

scenario_name: Independent flight termination system activated, swarm safely terminated
given: Swarm experiences dual failure: C2 link loss + autonomy fault; independent termination system activates per geofence trigger
when: On-board termination trigger fires (geofence breach or energy threshold)
then: All aircraft simultaneously power down and initiate safe descent; recovery is complete within impact zone
and: Termination event logged; case officer alerted; incident classified as CONTROLLED_TERMINATION

scenario_name: Contingency matrix executed - link loss → pre-planned waypoint navigation
given: C2 link lost during flight; contingency matrix specifies response: 'link loss > 10s → navigate to nearest safe landing waypoint'
when: Swarm recognizes link loss and timeout triggers contingency navigation
then: Pre-planned waypoint sequence executes; swarm navigates autonomously to safe landing site per ConOps
and: Contingency execution logged; post-flight analysis confirms waypoint selection was safe; incident classified as CONTINGENCY_SUCCESS

scenario_name: Contingency matrix gap - scenario not covered
given: Incident occurs that is not enumerated in contingency matrix (e.g., unexpected swarm member incapacitation while others are airborne)
when: Crew attempts to follow contingency procedures but finds no matching scenario
then: Crew must improvise; flight outcome is uncertain; post-incident review reveals gap in contingency planning
and: Case finding: contingency matrix is incomplete; design must include more comprehensive off-nominal scenario enumeration

scenario_name: ATSU coordination completed pre-flight, trial can commence
given: Trial operator has coordinated with ATSU; TDA is activated, NOTAM issued, and ATC is aware of trial
when: Flight commences within TDA boundaries
then: ATSU maintains awareness; any conflicting traffic is diverted outside TDA; trial proceeds without air traffic conflict
and: ATSU coordination log recorded; case file documents ATC liaison

scenario_name: Off-nominal state: swarm member loses altitude rapidly (structural failure suspected)
given: During flight, one swarm member reports rapid altitude loss; structural damage or battery failure suspected
when: Swarm de-confliction algorithm detects the failing member
then: Remaining swarm members immediately increase separation; failing member's trajectory is calculated; emergency termination considered if landing is not viable
and: Incident logged; off-nominal response tested post-flight; contingency procedure revised if inadequate

feature: F13: Incident Data Recording & MOR Handling
scenario_name: Complete recorded evidence captured, incident investigated, MOR filed
given: During trial, a minor incident occurs (e.g., unexplained brief altitude drop); complete flight data is recorded continuously at 100Hz for aircraft state, crew commands, autonomy decisions, and external events
when: Post-flight analysis begins; investigator reviews recorded data
then: Complete dataset enables root cause analysis; incident is classified for MOR threshold assessment
and: If MOR-reportable, formal report is filed with CAA within required timeframe; data archive is retained per regulations

feature: F13: Incident Data Recording & MOR Handling
scenario_name: Data recording gap during critical event - investigative gap
given: Incident occurs (near-collision); post-flight review discovers recording was interrupted due to disk full or sensor failure
when: Investigator attempts to reconstruct incident sequence
then: Critical time-window of data is absent; investigator cannot confidently determine root cause
and: Finding: data recording completeness is insufficient; design must ensure continuous recording with redundancy

feature: F13: Incident Data Recording & MOR Handling
scenario_name: DPIA compliance verified - no privacy violations
given: DPIA documentation specifies which personal data is collected (crew IDs, ground observer IDs), retention duration (30 days), and purpose (incident investigation only)
when: Case officer reviews DPIA against actual data collection procedures
then: DPIA is accurate and compliant with GDPR; personal data is minimized and protected
and: DPIA marked APPROVED; privacy compliance confirmed in case file

feature: F13: Incident Data Recording & MOR Handling
scenario_name: MOR obligation threshold ambiguous - uncertain filing requirement
given: Incident occurs that is borderline MOR-reportable (e.g., mild structural damage, no injury); guidance is unclear on threshold
when: Applicant/operator must decide whether to file MOR
then: Conservative approach taken: MOR is filed to be safe; CAA reviews and either classifies as MOR or closes as non-reportable
and: Post-incident, applicant requests clarification on future threshold; case log documents ambiguity resolved

scenario_name: Insurance policy verified, third-party risk transfer confirmed
given: Applicant submits insurance certificate from approved provider; coverage limit is £10M; exclusions are reviewed
when: Authorisation officer validates insurance documents
then: Insurance is verified as in-force, coverage is adequate for trial scope, exclusions are acceptable
and: Insurance marked APPROVED; policy details archived in case file; third-party risk is transferable

scenario_name: Insurance coverage insufficient or excludes UAS operations
given: Insurance policy submitted is for general aviation; exclusion clause states 'unmanned aircraft systems excluded from coverage'
when: Authorisation officer reviews policy details
then: Insurance is flagged as INADEQUATE; policy does not cover trial operations
and: Applicant must obtain UAS-specific insurance with adequate coverage; case cannot proceed without proper insurance

scenario_name: Ground survey confirms low population density, risk assessment factually grounded
given: Ground survey documents trial area: rural location, nearest residence 2km away, no schools or hospitals within 5km, no critical infrastructure overhead
when: Authorisation officer reviews survey data and risk assessment
then: Risk assessment is factually grounded in observed conditions; third-party exposure is low; survey is marked APPROVED
and: Survey geospatial data (population density, hazards) is parsed and overlaid on TDA map; risk justification confirmed

scenario_name: Ground survey missing hazard identification - risk assessment incomplete
given: Ground survey is submitted but does not identify power transmission lines, cellular towers, or critical facilities near trial area
when: Authorisation officer reviews survey completeness
then: Survey is flagged as INCOMPLETE; critical hazards are not documented
and: Applicant must conduct supplementary survey to identify all hazards; risk assessment cannot be finalized until complete

feature: F16: Crew Training & Competency Evidence Review
scenario_name: Crew credentials verified, training evidence demonstrates competency for swarm operations
given: Pilot credentials include: (1) valid commercial UAS license, (2) type rating for swarm aircraft, (3) completion of swarm-specific training course, (4) simulator checkride evidence
when: Licensing inspector reviews crew credentials and training records
then: All competency domains are verified; crew is qualified for trial scope; credentials marked APPROVED
and: Competency evidence is archived in case file; training records linked for future reference

feature: F16: Crew Training & Competency Evidence Review
scenario_name: Pilot has UAS license but lacks swarm operations experience
given: Pilot holds commercial UAS license but training records show no swarm-specific instruction or type rating
when: Licensing inspector assesses crew competency
then: Inspector flags missing swarm operations qualification; crew is not approved for swarm trial
and: Applicant must enroll pilot in swarm operations training course; case cannot proceed until competency is demonstrated

feature: F16: Crew Training & Competency Evidence Review
scenario_name: Crew training records incomplete or not current
given: Crew training completed 5 years ago; no recent recurrency training or checkride on file
when: Licensing inspector reviews training recency
then: Inspector flags training as STALE; competency currency cannot be assumed
and: Applicant must provide evidence of recent recurrency training (within past 12 months); case cannot open without current credentials

feature: F17: CAA/MAA Apportionment & Duty Holder Signature
scenario_name: Apportionment signed by both CAA and MAA, regulatory ownership gap-free
given: Apportionment document specifies: CAA owns airspace segregation and DAA assurance; MAA owns classified evidence handling and security compliance
when: Document is signed by authorized CAA and MAA representatives
then: Regulatory ownership is clarified and gap-free; both parties acknowledge their responsibilities
and: Signed apportionment is archived in case file; regulatory interface is documented

feature: F17: CAA/MAA Apportionment & Duty Holder Signature
scenario_name: Apportionment unsigned - regulatory authority unclear
given: Apportionment document is drafted but neither CAA nor MAA has signed; responsibility for classified evidence handling is ambiguous
when: Case officer requires signed apportionment before case opening
then: Case is blocked; apportionment must be signed by both parties before proceeding
and: Applicant is directed to obtain signatures; case remains in AWAIT_APPORTIONMENT status

feature: F17: CAA/MAA Apportionment & Duty Holder Signature
scenario_name: Apportionment gap identified - shared responsibility unclear
given: Apportionment specifies CAA responsibility for airspace and MAA responsibility for defense aspects, but classified evidence custody (CAA? MAA? Joint?) is unspecified
when: Case officer reviews signed apportionment
then: Gap is identified; responsibility allocation is incomplete
and: Apportionment must be revised and re-signed before case can proceed; gap-free ownership is required

feature: F18: Phased Trial Gates & Progressive Evidence Build
scenario_name: Phase 1 gate passed - evidence complete, Phase 2 authorized
given: Phase 1 is defined as: max 2 aircraft, pre-planned waypoints, segregated airspace, no autonomous decision-making; evidence threshold for Phase 1 is: 10 successful flights, zero incidents
when: Applicant requests Phase 2 progression after completing Phase 1 evidence requirements
then: Independent panel reviews Phase 1 evidence (10 flight logs, post-flight summaries, sensor data); all criteria met
and: Panel approves Phase 2 progression; Phase 2 (max 4 aircraft, increased autonomy) is authorized; evidence begins for Phase 2

feature: F18: Phased Trial Gates & Progressive Evidence Build
scenario_name: Phase gate failed - incident occurred, phase reversal required
given: During Phase 2 flights, a near-miss incident occurs involving geofence malfunction; evidence suggests Phase 2 autonomy level is not adequately validated
when: Panel reviews incident and Phase 2 evidence completeness
then: Panel fails Phase 2 gate due to incident; Phase 2 authorization is withdrawn
and: Trial reverts to Phase 1 operations; applicant must investigate incident, redesign containment logic, and accumulate additional evidence before Phase 2 re-attempt

feature: F18: Phased Trial Gates & Progressive Evidence Build
scenario_name: Phase gate re-run after remediation - evidence rebuild successful
given: Phase 2 gate initially failed due to geofence issue; applicant has redesigned geofence logic and conducted 10 new validation flights
when: Applicant resubmits Phase 2 evidence after remediation
then: Panel reviews new evidence (10 additional flights, geofence redesign analysis); all pass criteria are met
and: Phase 2 gate is re-passed; Phase 2 authorization is restored; trial progression continues

feature: F18: Phased Trial Gates & Progressive Evidence Build
scenario_name: Phased trial gates with evidence type matrices
given: Trial structure defines 3 phases with cumulative evidence requirements
when: Each phase gate is assessed against required evidence portfolio
then: Evidence sufficiency is evaluated per data-driven criteria
and: Scenario data:
scenario_data:
| Phase | Autonomy | Aircraft | Evidence Type | Quantity | Gate Pass Criteria |
| 1 | None | 2 | Flight logs | 10 | 10 successful flights, 0 incidents |
| 2 | Low | 4 | Flight logs + autonomy logs | 15 | 15 flights, autonomy behavior nominal |
| 3 | Medium | 6 | Full data + emergent behavior analysis | 20 | 20 flights, emergent behavior benign |

scenario_name: CAP 1616 consultation completed, stakeholders notified, community engagement documented
given: Trial operator has conducted CAP 1616 consultation: identified landowners within 1km, held community meeting, issued press release, addressed stakeholder concerns
when: Case officer reviews consultation documentation
then: Consultation is complete and documented; stakeholder feedback is recorded; trial proceeds with community awareness
and: Consultation log archived in case file; demonstrates transparency and regulatory compliance

scenario_name: REQ017 absent - community consultation not executed, regulatory gap
given: REQ017 (public/stakeholder engagement) is absent from user stories but is implied by CAP 1616 and GDPR
when: Trial proceeds without documented consultation
then: System behavior is undefined: does case open anyway? Is consultation deferred to post-flight? Or is case blocked?
and: CRITICAL FINDING: REQ017 must be explicitly addressed; system behavior for community engagement missing

scenario_name: Classified MOD evidence submitted, CAP 722H / JSP 440 procedures followed
given: Trial involves classified defense aspects; MOD evidence is marked OFFICIAL SENSITIVE; CAA and MOD have agreed compartmented handling procedures per CAP 722H / JSP 440
when: Classified evidence is submitted to CAA under secure custody arrangement
then: Evidence is tagged classified, stored in restricted access area, reviewed only by CAA security-cleared staff
and: CAA assurance case references classified evidence without disclosing content; regulatory defensibility is maintained without security breach

scenario_name: REQ018 absent - classified handling procedure undefined, regulatory/security gap
given: REQ018 (classification handling) is absent from user stories but is critical for defense trials
when: Trial involves classified evidence; system must handle it securely
then: System behavior is undefined: how are classified docs ingested, stored, accessed? Which staff are authorized? What is disclosure protocol?
and: CRITICAL FINDING: REQ018 must be explicitly specified; classification handling system is absent

scenario_name: Environmental impact assessment complete - noise, species, fire risk documented
given: Trial area has environmental survey identifying: bird nesting sites (dusk sensitivity), noise-sensitive species, battery fire risk mitigation plan
when: Environmental assessor reviews impact documentation
then: Environmental impacts are assessed; mitigation measures are documented; compliance with CAP 722 environmental obligations confirmed
and: Assessment marked APPROVED; environmental evidence archived in case file

scenario_name: REQ019 absent - environmental impact not assessed, regulatory gap
given: REQ019 (environmental impact) is absent from user stories but is critical for CAA authorization
when: Trial environment includes noise-sensitive or ecologically-sensitive areas
then: System behavior is undefined: does case require environmental assessment? What is assessment scope? Who reviews?
and: CRITICAL FINDING: REQ019 must be explicitly addressed; environmental assessment framework is absent

scenario_name: Trial concludes, lessons-learned captured, evidence archived, authorization closed
given: Trial completes successfully; all flights are concluded; post-flight analysis is complete
when: Case officer initiates close-out process; lessons-learned are compiled
then: Final flight data is archived in immutable format; lessons-learned report documents key findings, operational insights, and design improvements for future trials
and: Authorization is formally closed; case transitions to CLOSED_COMPLETE status; institutional learning is captured

scenario_name: REQ020 absent - post-trial close-out procedure undefined, evidence preservation gap
given: REQ020 (post-trial close-out) is absent from user stories
when: Trial concludes; case must close
then: System behavior is undefined: what is close-out procedure? How is evidence preserved? Is lessons-learned report required? What is retention policy?
and: CRITICAL FINDING: REQ020 must be explicitly addressed; post-trial close-out framework is absent

feature: F02, F18: Completeness Check + Phased Gates
scenario_name: Intake completeness applies to each phase gate, not just initial application
given: Trial structure requires completeness validation at each phase gate transition, not just intake
when: Each phase progression is assessed
then: Evidence completeness for that phase is validated; missing artefacts block phase progression
and: Scenario data:
scenario_data:
| Gate | Required Completeness Items | Status Check |
| Intake | ConOps, ODD, SORA, insurance, crew credentials, geofence design | All present |
| Phase 2 | Autonomy architecture, swarm de-confliction design, Phase 1 flight evidence | All present before Gate 2 |
| Phase 3 | Emergent behavior analysis, expanded ConOps, additional crew certification | All present before Gate 3 |
